questions.question: What is the primary function of a Hypervisor?
options: A) To manage physical hardware components
B) To create and manage virtual machines
C) To manage the operating system and applications
questions.explanation: A Hypervisor is a piece of software that creates and manages virtual machines, allowing multiple operating systems to run on a single physical host.

questions.question: Which of the following is a type of virtualization mechanism?
questions.explanation: Virtualization of CPU, memory and I/O devices are all types of virtualization mechanisms, which allow multiple virtual machines to run on a single physical host.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a Virtual Cluster?
options: A) To manage physical hardware resources
C) To provide high availability and scalability
questions.explanation: A Virtual Cluster is a collection of virtual machines that can be managed together to provide high availability and scalability.

questions.question: Which tool is used to manage virtual machines and resources?
questions.explanation: A Hypervisor is a tool used to manage virtual machines and resources, allowing multiple operating systems to run on a single physical host.

questions.question: What is the benefit of virtualizing I/O devices?
questions.explanation: Virtualizing I/O devices allows for better resource utilization, as multiple virtual machines can share the same physical devices, improving overall system efficiency.
